data.title: Fifth District Bancorp 2025 10-K: $22.058M Interest Income, $0.80 EPS
data.description:
Fifth District Bancorp reported a strong turnaround in its 2025 results, driven by higher yields and larger loan and investment balances that lifted total interest and dividend income to $22.058 million and produced net income of $4.088 million. Improved net interest margin drove net interest income (reported as gross profit) to $12.848 million, and diluted and basic earnings per share rose to $0.80. Results were also aided by a $3.5 million gain on insurance proceeds and other one-time gains that contributed to income before taxes of $4.185 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $22.058 million, up $2.8 million (14.4%) versus 2024 due to higher yields and larger investment and loan balances.
  • Gross Profit (reported as Net Interest Income): $12.848 million, up $2.792 million versus 2024 reflecting improved net interest margin.
  • Income (Loss) Before Income Taxes: $4.185 million, a turnaround from a $(1.436) million loss in 2024 driven by higher interest income and one-time gains.
  • Net Income: $4.088 million, versus a $(1.078) million loss in 2024; benefited from a $3.5 million gain on insurance proceeds and higher net interest income.
  • Income (Loss) per Share - Basic and Diluted: $0.80, improved from $(0.21) in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth & Margin Improvement: Net interest income rose 27.8% year-over-year driven by higher loan yields and reinvestment in higher-yielding securities.
  • Channel & Product Mix Shift: Continued focus on fixed-rate 1-4 family mortgages to retain portfolio while moderately growing commercial lending and participations.
  • Deposit & Funding Trends: Ongoing efforts to grow core deposits with a modest shift from money market accounts to certificates of deposit and NOW accounts to support liquidity.
  • Operational Momentum & Service Model: Emphasis on community-oriented, full-service loan servicing and conservative underwriting to sustain asset quality.
  • Capital & Liquidity Posture: Maintained strong liquidity and elevated capital levels to support organic growth and opportunistic branching and acquisitions.

Original SEC Filing: Fifth District Bancorp, Inc. [ FDSB ] - 10-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Rio Tinto Secures Deal to Keep Australia's Second Largest Aluminum Smelter Open
data.description:
By David Winning

SYDNEY--Rio Tinto said it had reached a deal with lawmakers to keep open its Boyne aluminum smelter in eastern Australia, seeking to insulate the operation from rising prices of fossil fuels by leaning more heavily on renewable power.

Rio Tinto said the agreement with the Queensland state government and the federal government builds on power purchase agreements it has signed in recent years to underwrite 7.5 billion Australian dollars (US$5.25 billion) in new renewable energy and storage in the state.

The Queensland and federal governments have agreed to invest a combined A$2 billion over the ten years through 2040, seeking to support the Boyne smelter's transition to new power supply once its current electricity contract ends in 2029.

Boyne is Australia's second largest aluminum smelter and has been in operation since 1982. It is majority owned by Rio Tinto and employs 1,000 people.

"As fossil fuels become increasingly expensive, this investment, combined with the power purchase agreements we have already signed, positions Boyne to be among the world's first aluminum smelters underpinned by solar and wind power," said Jérôme Pécresse, head of Rio Tinto's aluminum and lithium business.

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com

data.title: Amphenol Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >APH
data.description:
Amphenol Corp. (APH) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 24, 2026.

On March 24, 2026, Amphenol Technologies Holding GmbH ("Amphenol Technologies"), a German limited liability company (Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung) and wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of Amphenol Corporation (the "Company"), entered into an underwriting agreement (the "Amphenol Technologies Underwriting Agreement") by and among Amphenol Technologies, as issuer, the Company, as guarantor, and Barclays Bank PLC, Citigroup Global Markets Europe AG, Commerzbank Aktiengesellschaft, HSBC Bank plc, BofA Securities Europe SA, Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, TD Global Finance unlimited company, U.S. Bancorp Investments, Inc., Loop Capital Markets LLC and Siebert Williams Shank & Co., LLC, relating to the offer and sale of €500,000,000 aggregate principal amount of Amphenol Technologies' 3.625% Senior Notes due 2031 (the "Notes").

The closing of the Notes offering (the "Notes Offering") is expected to occur on March 30, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

A copy of the Amphenol Technologies Underwriting Agreement is attached hereto as Exhibit 1.1 and is filed herewith for purposes of incorporation by reference into the Company's Registration Statement (No. 333-293923).

The above description of the Amphenol Technologies Underwriting Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference to the Amphenol Technologies Underwriting Agreement, a copy of which is attached as Exhibit 1.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and is incorporated by reference herein.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/820313/000110465926033968/tm267510d9_8k.htm

data.title: Baltimore sues Elon Musk's xAI over Grok sexual 'deepfakes'
data.description:
Key points:
  • Baltimore says Grok generates nonconsensual explicit images
  • Grok faces regulatory probes in multiple countries
  • xAI, parent SpaceX not available for comment

By Jonathan Stempel

The city of Baltimore sued Elon Musk’s xAI on Tuesday, claiming its Grok chatbot illegally generates nonconsensual sexually explicit images, including of children.

Baltimore, with a population of about 568,000, is the largest city to sue xAI over "deepfakes" attributed to Grok, its lawyers said.

Musk launched Grok in 2023 and distributes it through his social media platform X, which like xAI is now part of his rocket and space exploration company SpaceX.

Neither SpaceX nor xAI immediately responded to requests for comment.

The Maryland city said in a complaint filed in Baltimore Circuit Court that xAI is violating its consumer protection statute by promoting Grok as a safe, general-purpose artificial intelligence assistant for everyday people.

Baltimore said Grok has flooded X users with objectionable content, becoming one of the largest distributors of material depicting nonconsensual sexual activity and child sexual abuse despite promising it bans such content and permits only consensually produced adult nudity.

The city also said X, formerly known as Twitter, generated an estimated 3 million realistic-looking sexualized images --including more than 23,000 of children — over 11 days around the start of the year.

“We’re talking about tech companies enabling the sexual exploitation of children," Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott said in a statement. "It's a threat to privacy, dignity and public safety, and those responsible must be held accountable."

REGULATORS IN SEVERAL COUNTRIES PROBING GROK

Musk's xAI faces regulatory probes in several countries in the Americas, Europe, Asia and in Australia over Grok.

In mid-January, xAI said it restricted image editing in Grok, and blocked users from generating images of people in revealing clothing in "jurisdictions where it's illegal."

Musk said at the time he was "not aware of any naked underage images generated by Grok. Literally zero."

The complaint includes a Grok-generated image that Musk shared on December 31, 2025, depicting the 54-year-old in a blue string bikini. Baltimore called it a "public endorsement" of Grok’s ability to generate revealing edits of real people.

The 3 million estimate came from the Center for Countering Digital Hate, based on a random sample of 20,000 images that Grok generated.

Baltimore is seeking an injunction requiring xAI to change Grok's "exploitative" design features, and pay unspecified fines.

Last month's combination of SpaceX and xAI created the world's most valuable private company, worth about $1.25 trillion at the time.

data.title: DigitalOcean Holdings Shares Fall on $700M Public Offering
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of DigitalOcean Holdings fell after the company said it would raise $700 million through a public offering of shares.

Shares fell 9% to $77.31 in after-hours trading Tuesday after closing at $84.92. At close, the stock had more than doubled over the past year.

The cloud-infrastructure company said it will use proceeds from the offering to expand its infrastructure capacity to meet demand, pay down an existing term loan, and for other general corporate purposes.

The underwriters for the offering have a 30-day option to buy an additional $105 million of stock from the company.

JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Bank of America Securities are joint book-running managers.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Devon Energy Extends Credit Facility to 2031 and Removes 10 bps SOFR Spread
data.description:
Devon Energy entered into a First Amendment to its Amended and Restated Credit Agreement to extend the facility’s maturity and lower borrowing costs. The amendment pushes the maturity to March 24, 2031, renews Devon’s option to seek up to three one-year extensions with majority lender consent, and removes the 10 basis point credit spread adjustment to SOFR-based rates. The company expects the changes to enhance liquidity and reduce interest expense.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (revolving credit facility)
  • Counterparty: Bank of America, as administrative agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until Mar 24 2031, with up to three one-year extensions subject to consent
  • Reason: Extend maturity and reduce borrowing costs

Original SEC Filing: DEVON ENERGY CORP/DE [ DVN ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: CytoDyn Extends $57M Convertible Notes to 2029; 5% Rate, $1M Monthly Stock Payments
data.description:
CytoDyn amended and extended two secured convertible promissory notes totaling $57.0 million, pushing their maturities to April 2029. The company will make $1,000,000 in monthly payments in common stock across both notes, priced at the lower of the prior closing price or the five-day average. The annual interest rate on each note was reduced to 5%, improving the overall cost of capital and liquidity profile. The extensions were agreed with the Noteholders on Mar 24, 2026.

Agreement 1: CytoDyn Extends $28.5M Convertible Note to 2029; Rate Cut to 5%, Stock Payments

  • Agreement type: Amendment and extension of secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Noteholders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 36 months (to Apr 5 2029)
  • Reason: Extend maturities and reduce interest expense

Agreement 2: CytoDyn Extends Second $28.5M Convertible Note to 2029 With 5% Interest

  • Agreement type: Amendment and extension of secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Noteholders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 36 months (to Apr 23 2029)
  • Reason: Extend maturities and reduce interest expense

Original SEC Filing: CytoDyn Inc. [ CYDY ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: Caring Brands Raises $3.6M PIPE, Adds Registration Rights and Retires 6.25M Insider Shares
data.description:
Caring Brands secured $3.6 million through a PIPE financing, issuing Series A Convertible Preferred Stock and 9.47 million five-year warrants at a $0.40 exercise price. The preferred stock converts at $0.40 per share and includes ownership caps and a 19.99% issuance limit without shareholder approval; the investor also has an additional $4.0 million investment right with pricing protections. The company entered a Registration Rights Agreement to register the resale of conversion and warrant shares. Caring Brands will use $3.075 million of proceeds to repurchase 6.25 million insider shares, cutting outstanding shares to approximately 8.51 million, with the balance for general corporate purposes.

Agreement 1: Caring Brands Raises $3.6 Million in PIPE; Issues Series A Preferred and 9.47M Warrants

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for PIPE financing (Series A Preferred and Common Warrants)
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor Purchaser
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Strengthen liquidity and fund share redemption and working capital

Agreement 2: Caring Brands Signs Registration Rights Deal to Register Preferred Conversion and Warrant Shares

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement for resale registration
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor Purchaser
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Provide resale liquidity to investor

Agreement 3: Caring Brands to Retire 6.25M Shares From Insiders Using $3.075 Million of PIPE Proceeds

  • Agreement type: Share Redemption Agreements to repurchase insider-held common stock
  • Counterparty: Certain Insiders (Dr. Glynn Wilson; Brnan John; NovoDX)
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Reduce share count and align insider holdings

Original SEC Filing: Caring Brands, Inc. [ CABR ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

data.title: BCP Investment Raises $50 Million in 7.50% Notes Due 2029 to Refinance Debt
data.description:
BCP Investment entered into financing agreements supporting a $50 million offering of 7.50% notes due 2029, with proceeds earmarked to repay $40 million of 5.25% notes due 2026 and to pay down other indebtedness. The company signed a Note Purchase Agreement with purchasers on March 20, 2026 and a Sixth Supplemental Indenture with U.S. Bank Trust Company on March 24, 2026 to establish terms and covenants. The notes pay interest semi-annually and feature a make-whole call prior to April 24, 2029 and a par call on or after that date, improving liquidity and extending maturities.

Agreement 1: BCP Investment Raises $50 Million in 7.50% Notes Due 2029 to Refinance Debt

  • Agreement type: Note Purchase Agreement for 7.50% notes due 2029
  • Counterparty: Purchasers named in the Note Purchase Agreement
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / Mar 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Sep 24 2029
  • Reason: Refinance 2026 notes and reduce other indebtedness

Agreement 2: BCP Investment Executes Sixth Supplemental Indenture for 7.50% Notes Due 2029

  • Agreement type: Sixth Supplemental Indenture governing 7.50% notes due 2029
  • Counterparty: U.S. Bank Trust Company
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / Mar 24 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Sep 24 2029
  • Reason: Set terms and covenants for the new notes

data.title: PPL says FERC sets New England transmission ROE at 9.57%, refunds ordered
data.description:
PPL said FERC set a new ROE methodology for New England transmission owners, establishing a base ROE of 9.57% and maximum with incentives of 12.09%, retroactive to Oct. 16, 2014.

Key Highlights:

  • FERC issued Opinion No. 594 setting NETOs' base ROE at 9.57% and max with incentives at 12.09%, retroactive to Oct. 16, 2014.
  • FERC ordered refunds with interest for certain periods affected by the decision, impacting Rhode Island Energy.
  • PPL and Rhode Island Energy are evaluating next steps, including potential coordination with other NETOs and possible appeal.
  • PPL does not expect the Order to have a material impact and reaffirms its 2026 EPS forecast of $1.90–$1.98 and 6%–8% annual EPS growth target.

Original SEC Filing: PPL Corp [ PPL ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Janus Henderson Lifts Merger Price to $52 Per Share in Amendment With Jupiter
data.description:
Janus Henderson entered into Amendment No. 1 to its Agreement and Plan of Merger with Jupiter Company and Jupiter Merger Sub, raising the cash consideration to $52.00 per share. The amendment also revises termination-related payments, including a fixed $118.2 million expense reimbursement if the required vote fails, and increases potential termination fees. It permits quarterly dividends up to $1.00 per share for quarters starting on or after July 1, 2026, subject to key approvals, and allows post-vote employee rollover opportunities. The merger would result in Janus Henderson becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of Jupiter Company.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment No. 1 to Agreement and Plan of Merger
  • Counterparty: Jupiter Company and Jupiter Merger Sub
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing of merger
  • Reason: Enhance deal terms and advance merger closing

Original SEC Filing: JANUS HENDERSON GROUP PLC [ JHG ] - 8-K - Mar. 24, 2026
